nodes.context: Large and powerful drug trafficking transnational organized crime syndicate based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, Mexico.
Co-founded by Félix Gallardo between 1978 and 1980, controlled much of Mexico's drug-trafficking corridors along the U.S. border throughout the 1980s.
Rival cartel that emerged from the Guadalajara Cartel, controlled Tijuana smuggling route.
Rival cartel that controlled some of eastern Mexico's drug trade.

edges.evidence_quote: Following Gallardo's arrest in 1989, the cartel splintered into smaller organizations, including the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel was a splinter of the Guadalajara Cartel after its collapse.

edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa Cartel has been waging a war against the Tijuana Cartel (Arellano-Félix Organization) over the Tijuana smuggling route to the border city of San Diego, California.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel and Tijuana Cartel are in a violent conflict over smuggling routes.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel was heavily involved in violent conflicts with rival groups such as the Tijuana Cartel, the Gulf Cartel, and later, the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), as well as with Mexican federal forces.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel was in violent conflict with Gulf Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel was heavily involved in violent conflicts with rival groups such as the Tijuana Cartel, the Gulf Cartel, and later, the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), as well as with Mexican federal forces.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel was in violent conflict with Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Zambada also helped Amado Carrillo Fuentes expand the Juárez Cartel in the state of Chihuahua and helped incorporate some of the remnants of the Juárez Cartel into the Sinaloa Cartel after Carrillo's death in 1997. The Sinaloa Cartel was therefore believed to be linked to the Juárez Cartel in a strategic alliance following the partnership of their rivals, the Gulf Cartel and Tijuana Cartel.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel had a strategic alliance with Juárez Cartel and later incorporated its remnants.

edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa Cartel was partially splintered in 2008 when the Beltrán-Leyva brothers broke apart from the cartel.
edges.context: Beltrán-Leyva brothers were formerly part of Sinaloa Cartel and later broke away.

edges.evidence_quote: The Milenio (Michoacán) , Jalisco (Guadalajara), Sonora (Sonora) , and Colima cartels were now branches of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Milenio Cartel became a branch of Sinaloa Cartel by 2006.

edges.evidence_quote: The Milenio (Michoacán) , Jalisco (Guadalajara), Sonora (Sonora) , and Colima cartels were now branches of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Colima Cartel became a branch of Sinaloa Cartel by 2006.

edges.evidence_quote: The Milenio (Michoacán) , Jalisco (Guadalajara), Sonora (Sonora) , and Colima cartels were now branches of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Sonora Cartel became a branch of Sinaloa Cartel by 2006.

edges.evidence_quote: the Arellano-Félix brothers successfully escaping from the location with the assistance of David Barron Corona , a member of the Logan Heights Gang .
edges.context: Logan Heights Gang assisted Tijuana Cartel members in escaping a shootout.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Negros have been known to employ gangs such as the Mara Salvatrucha to carry out murders and other illegal activities.
edges.context: Los Negros are employed by the Sinaloa Cartel to carry out murders and other illegal activities.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Negros have been known to employ gangs such as the Mara Salvatrucha to carry out murders and other illegal activities.
edges.context: Los Negros employ Mara Salvatrucha to carry out murders and other illegal activities.

edges.evidence_quote: Following the 2003 arrest of Gulf Cartel leader Osiel Cárdenas Guillén, it is believed the Sinaloa Cartel moved 200 men into the region to battle the Gulf Cartel for control.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel moved men to battle the Gulf Cartel for control of the Nuevo Laredo drug trafficking corridor.

edges.evidence_quote: The shipments were mostly bought from the Sinaloa Cartel and at times from the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel, and it is assumed that both cartels threatened the Flores crew with violence if they bought from other rival drug organizations.
edges.context: Both cartels supplied the Flores crew and threatened them with violence if they bought from rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: He was a leader and founding member of Los Ántrax, an armed squadron formed to protect Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada García, founding member of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Los Ántrax is an armed squadron formed to protect a founding member of the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: As of November 2022, the Chapitos and Zambada factions are rumored to have reconciled their recent differences in order to come together to battle the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel factions reconciled to battle the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The municipalities of Cosala and Mazatlán are said to have more than one group controlling the territory as well as some of the northern regions of the state, which are purportedly partially controlled by the Beltrán-Leyva Organization.
edges.context: Beltrán-Leyva Organization partially controls some northern regions of Sinaloa state, alongside Sinaloa Cartel factions.

edges.evidence_quote: In the late 2000s and early 2010s, the Sinaloa Cartel and Gulf Cartel were traditionally considered allies but as of 2021, this alliance has reportedly fizzled out as the two groups now battle each other in the state of Zacatecas with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG) now siding with the Gulf to fight against Sinaloa.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel and Gulf Cartel were allies in late 2000s/early 2010s, but later became enemies.

edges.evidence_quote: In the late 2000s and early 2010s, the Sinaloa Cartel and Gulf Cartel were traditionally considered allies but as of 2021, this alliance has reportedly fizzled out as the two groups now battle each other in the state of Zacatecas with the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG) now siding with the Gulf to fight against Sinaloa.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel and Gulf Cartel battle each other in Zacatecas as of 2021.

edges.context: CJNG sides with Gulf Cartel to fight Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa Federation has also formed alliances with two powerful Chinese triads , Sun Yee On and the 14K Triad , to acquire the precursor chemicals needed in creating highly-addictive synthetic drugs like methamphetamine, and now, likely fentanyl.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel allies with Sun Yee On to acquire precursor chemicals.

edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa Federation has also formed alliances with two powerful Chinese triads , Sun Yee On and the 14K Triad , to acquire the precursor chemicals needed in creating highly-addictive synthetic drugs like methamphetamine, and now, likely fentanyl.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel allies with 14K Triad to acquire precursor chemicals.

edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa Cartel also reportedly has a major presence in Colombia and has partnered with various paramilitary drug cartels such as the Clan del Golfo .
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel partners with Clan del Golfo in Colombia.

edges.evidence_quote: such intel was instrumental to the takedown of the Tijuana Cartel, the Sinaloa cartel's main rival
edges.context: Tijuana Cartel is the main rival of Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the death of Arturo Beltrán Leyva, who led a splinter group from the Sinaloa cartel
edges.context: Beltrán-Leyva Organization is a splinter group from Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: During the 2000s and possibly still today, it is believed that a group known as the Herrera Organization would transport multi-ton quantities of cocaine from South America to Guatemala on behalf of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Herrera Organization transported cocaine on behalf of Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Other shipments of South American cocaine are believed to originate directly from Cali and Medellín drug-trafficking groups in Colombia, from which the Sinaloa Cartel then handles transportation across the U.S. border
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel obtains cocaine from Cali Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Other shipments of South American cocaine are believed to originate directly from Cali and Medellín drug-trafficking groups in Colombia, from which the Sinaloa Cartel then handles transportation across the U.S. border
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel obtains cocaine from Medellín Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: the Sinaloa Cartel is a major player in the global cocaine trade operating within Colombia where it helps finance and support several of Colombia's local paramilitary drug-trafficking groups such as the left-wing National Liberation Army
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel finances and supports the National Liberation Army.

edges.evidence_quote: the Sinaloa Cartel is a major player in the global cocaine trade operating within Colombia where it helps finance and support several of Colombia's local paramilitary drug-trafficking groups such as ... dissidents of FARC
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel finances and supports FARC dissidents.

edges.evidence_quote: the Sinaloa Cartel is a major player in the global cocaine trade operating within Colombia where it helps finance and support several of Colombia's local paramilitary drug-trafficking groups such as ... Colombia's current largest drug cartel and right-wing paramilitary group; the Clan del Golfo
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel finances and supports the Clan del Golfo.

edges.evidence_quote: the CDS and CJNG rivalry has been detected, which has reportedly led to the displacement of thousands of Colombian citizens just in early 2021
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel and CJNG are rivals in Colombia.

edges.evidence_quote: The CDS is said to have a longstanding partnership with one of Ecuador's largest and most powerful criminal groups; The Choneros
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel has a longstanding partnership with Los Choneros.

edges.evidence_quote: Factions like the Chapitos have already gotten into 'branding' of cannabis (marijuana) products and investing in production as well as lobbying certain politicians for legalization
edges.context: Chapitos is a faction of the Sinaloa Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The evolution of trafficking routes from Colombian cartels to Mexican drug trafficking organizations (DTOs) shifted the dynamics, impacting Latin American territories. Specifically, the competition between Colombian cartels and Mexican DTOs, as well as internal competition among Mexican groups, contributed to a surge in violence over the past few decades.
edges.context: Sinaloa Cartel competed with Colombian cartels for control of trafficking routes.

edges.evidence_quote: In Tom Clancy's 2011 novel Against All Enemies, the cartel is led by Ernesto "El Matador" Zuñiga, a brutal and powerful Mexican drug lord and rival of Jorge Rojas, the leader of the Juarez Cartel and the main antagonist of Against All Enemies.
edges.context: In the novel, the Sinaloa Cartel leader is a rival of the Juarez Cartel leader.

edges.evidence_quote: In the Netflix series Ozark, the Sinaloa Cartel is portrayed as the main rival of the fictional Navarro cartel in an ongoing war.
edges.context: In the series, the Sinaloa Cartel is at war with the Navarro cartel.
